title: Snap Q2 2023 Earnings Report Summary
text:
Snap Q2 2023 Earnings Report Summary

Financial Performance

Snap exceeded Wall Street expectations in Q2 2023, delivering revenues of $1.07 billion against the anticipated $1.05 billion. The company narrowed quarterly losses to $377.3 million. However, year-over-year revenue declined 4 percent, and the company's stock tumbled after hours due to cautious forward guidance.

User Growth Metrics

The platform expanded its user base to 397 million daily active users, while Snapchat+ premium subscribers reached over 4 million.

Business Transition & Challenges

Management characterized their situation as undergoing rapid transition while enhancing their advertising platform. The company provided its first formal guidance since early 2022, projecting Q3 revenue growth between down 5 percent to flat year-over-year, citing limited advertising demand visibility.

Strategic Priorities

CEO Evan Spiegel outlined three key initiatives: sustaining community growth through product investment, expanding direct-response advertising to demonstrate measurable return, and developing alternative revenue streams for business resilience.

Cost Management

The company achieved an 8 percent operating expense reduction year-over-year while headcount decreased approximately 20 percent from mid-Q3 2022 peaks, though modest future hiring is anticipated in machine learning and AI development areas.
